content:
[EXAMPLE - quiz | en | Animals]
The following is an approved game example for schema, style, and factual grounding.

```json
{"game_type":"quiz","title":"Animal Classification Basics","difficulty_percentage":35,"questions":[{"question":"Which group of animals has a backbone?","options":["Vertebrates","Insects","Mollusks","Arachnids"],"correct_index":0,"explanation":"Vertebrates are animals with backbones. Insects, mollusks, and arachnids are invertebrate groups."},{"question":"Which statement correctly describes mammals?","options":["They are all aquatic","They usually have hair or fur and feed young with milk","They all lay hard-shelled eggs","They do not breathe air"],"correct_index":1,"explanation":"Mammals generally have hair or fur and females produce milk for their young, although there are rare egg-laying mammals."},{"question":"What is the main difference between vertebrates and invertebrates?","options":["Body color","Whether they have a backbone","Where they live","What they eat"],"correct_index":1,"explanation":"The presence or absence of a backbone is the key distinction."}]}
```

[EXAMPLE - quiz | en | Politics]
The following is an approved game example for schema, style, and factual grounding.

```json
{"game_type":"quiz","title":"Democracy and Government","difficulty_percentage":45,"questions":[{"question":"What does rule of law mean in a democratic system?","options":["Only leaders follow laws","Laws apply to people and government institutions","Courts are optional","Votes replace all rights"],"correct_index":1,"explanation":"Rule of law means laws constrain both citizens and government power."},{"question":"Which branch of government is usually responsible for interpreting laws?","options":["Judicial","Legislative","Executive","Military"],"correct_index":0,"explanation":"Judicial institutions interpret laws and resolve legal disputes."},{"question":"Why are checks and balances used?","options":["To remove elections","To prevent one branch from holding unchecked power","To end public debate","To replace courts with parties"],"correct_index":1,"explanation":"Checks and balances distribute authority and create limits between branches."}]}
```

[EXAMPLE - quiz | en | Science: Computers]
The following is an approved game example for schema, style, and factual grounding.

```json
{"game_type":"quiz","title":"Networks and the Web","difficulty_percentage":40,"questions":[{"question":"What is the internet?","options":["A single website","A global network of connected networks","Only Wi-Fi","A programming language"],"correct_index":1,"explanation":"The internet is a global network of networks that can carry many services, including the web."},{"question":"Which statement about Wi-Fi is accurate?","options":["It is a wireless local networking technology","It is the same thing as every website","It stores all internet pages","It replaces IP addressing"],"correct_index":0,"explanation":"Wi-Fi connects devices wirelessly on local networks."},{"question":"What does cybersecurity primarily protect?","options":["Only printed books","Systems, networks, and data","Weather forecasts","Physical exercise plans"],"correct_index":1,"explanation":"Cybersecurity focuses on protecting digital systems, networks, accounts, and data."}]}
```

[EXAMPLE - word-pass | en | Science & Nature]
The following is an approved game example for schema, style, and factual grounding.

```json
{"game_type":"word-pass","title":"Cell Biology Word Pass","difficulty_percentage":45,"words":[{"letter":"C","hint":"Basic unit of life.","answer":"Cell","starts_with":true},{"letter":"D","hint":"Molecule that stores genetic instructions.","answer":"DNA","starts_with":true},{"letter":"M","hint":"Organelle that releases usable energy in many eukaryotic cells.","answer":"Mitochondrion","starts_with":true},{"letter":"R","hint":"Cell structure that builds proteins.","answer":"Ribosome","starts_with":true},{"letter":"N","hint":"Structure that contains DNA in eukaryotic cells.","answer":"Nucleus","starts_with":true}]}
```

[EXAMPLE - word-pass | en | Entertainment: Music]
The following is an approved game example for schema, style, and factual grounding.

```json
{"game_type":"word-pass","title":"Music Vocabulary Word Pass","difficulty_percentage":40,"words":[{"letter":"T","hint":"Speed of a piece of music.","answer":"Tempo","starts_with":true},{"letter":"P","hint":"How high or low a sound is perceived.","answer":"Pitch","starts_with":true},{"letter":"R","hint":"Pattern of sounds and silences in time.","answer":"Rhythm","starts_with":true},{"letter":"B","hint":"Instrument family that uses lip vibration into a mouthpiece.","answer":"Brass","starts_with":true},{"letter":"S","hint":"Instrument family that produces sound from vibrating strings.","answer":"String","starts_with":true}]}
```

[EXAMPLE - word-pass | en | Geography]
The following is an approved game example for schema, style, and factual grounding.

```json
{"game_type":"word-pass","title":"Map Skills Word Pass","difficulty_percentage":35,"words":[{"letter":"L","hint":"North-south coordinate measured from the equator.","answer":"Latitude","starts_with":true},{"letter":"M","hint":"Visual representation of an area.","answer":"Map","starts_with":true},{"letter":"S","hint":"Map feature that compares map distance to real distance.","answer":"Scale","starts_with":true},{"letter":"D","hint":"Landform that can form when a river deposits sediment near its mouth.","answer":"Delta","starts_with":true},{"letter":"T","hint":"Smaller river or stream that flows into a larger one.","answer":"Tributary","starts_with":true}]}
```

[EXAMPLE - true_false | en | Science: Mathematics]
The following is an approved game example for schema, style, and factual grounding.

```json
{"game_type":"true_false","title":"Statistics True or False","difficulty_percentage":35,"statements":[{"statement":"The median is the middle value when a data set is ordered.","is_true":true,"explanation":"The median is found by ordering values and locating the middle position."},{"statement":"The mean and the mode always have the same value.","is_true":false,"explanation":"The mean is an arithmetic average, while the mode is the most frequent value. They can differ."},{"statement":"A biased sample can lead to misleading conclusions.","is_true":true,"explanation":"If a sample does not represent the population well, conclusions may be distorted."},{"statement":"Equivalent fractions always have different values.","is_true":false,"explanation":"Equivalent fractions such as 1/2 and 2/4 represent the same value."}]}
```

[EXAMPLE - true_false | en | History]
The following is an approved game example for schema, style, and factual grounding.

```json
{"game_type":"true_false","title":"Roman Republic True or False","difficulty_percentage":50,"statements":[{"statement":"The Roman Republic existed before the Roman Empire.","is_true":true,"explanation":"The Republic preceded imperial rule in Roman history."},{"statement":"The Senate was one of the institutions of the Roman Republic.","is_true":true,"explanation":"The Senate was an important political body in the Republic."},{"statement":"Julius Caesar became important after the Republic had already become a modern democracy.","is_true":false,"explanation":"Caesar was part of the late Republican crisis, not a modern democratic system."},{"statement":"Roman citizenship and military expansion influenced Republican society.","is_true":true,"explanation":"Citizenship, conflict, and expansion all shaped the Roman Republic."}]}
```

[EXAMPLE - true_false | en | Entertainment: Video Games]
The following is an approved game example for schema, style, and factual grounding.

```json
{"game_type":"true_false","title":"Game Design True or False","difficulty_percentage":40,"statements":[{"statement":"A core gameplay loop describes repeated actions players perform during play.","is_true":true,"explanation":"Core loops capture recurring actions such as exploring, collecting, solving, or building."},{"statement":"Accessibility options can include subtitles and remappable controls.","is_true":true,"explanation":"Subtitles and remappable controls are common accessibility features."},{"statement":"Game mechanics are the same thing as hardware platforms.","is_true":false,"explanation":"Mechanics are rules and interactions; platforms are devices or environments that run games."},{"statement":"Many games combine more than one genre.","is_true":true,"explanation":"Hybrid games can mix puzzle, role-playing, action, strategy, and other patterns."}]}
```
